channel.title: Mack Lab @ UofT
channel.description: Recent content on Mack Lab @ UofT

item.title: Attentional Processes
item.description: Attention is a filtering mechanism that helps us focus on what matters. We investigate how attentional biases are formed and how they influence learning.

item.title: Category Learning
item.description: How do we learn to group items into categories? Our lab explores the underlying cognitive and neural processes involved in category acquisition.

item.title: Neural Mechanisms
item.description: Using fMRI and other neuroimaging techniques, we map the neural correlates of learning and memory.

item.title: New paper on functional footprints published in PNAS!
item.description:
Our latest research on functional footprints has been published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS).

Check it out to learn more about how attention and memory interact during learning!

item.title: Congratulations, Dr. Gumus!
item.description:
Giant congratulations to the newest Dr. in the lab, Dr. Gumus!

We are so proud of your hard work and successful defense. Well done!

item.title: Congratulations, Dr. Perovic!
item.description:
Another doctor in the house! Congratulations to Dr. Perovic on a successful defense.

Excellent work and a well-deserved result!

item.title: Dr. Michael Mack
item.description:
Michael Mack is an Associate Professor in the Department of Psychology at the University of Toronto.

His research interests include… (Add more detail here).

item.title: Join the Lab
item.description:
We are always looking for motivated undergraduate and graduate students, as well as postdoctoral fellows, who are interested in the intersection of attention and memory.

If you are interested in joining the Mack Lab, please send an email to Dr. Michael Mack (michael.mack@utoronto.ca) with your CV and a brief statement of your research interests.

item.title: Participate in Our Research
item.description:
Interested in participating in our studies? We are frequently looking for participants for our behavioral and neuroimaging experiments!

How to sign up
#

Please check out our current studies on the University of Toronto Psychology Participation Pool or contact us directly at [EMAIL_ADDRESS] for more information.
